acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_dialogue_01] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:289-295 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:403-500 clearly separates speaker texture on the page: Mira argues through record/process/cost language, while Lin's answers hinge on sensory recall, domestic detail, or concrete courtroom experience before any abstract claim. Fail if adjacent rebuttals still end on thesis-like maxims in both voices.

[chapter_16_prose_01] Pass if none of the three cited beats opens with the exact phrase 'There it was' and each revised turn is announced through distinct syntax tied to its immediate object, pressure, or sensation. Fail if two or more of those moments still rely on the same opener.

[chapter_16_prose_02] Pass if at least three of the cited crowd-reaction beats are rewritten to name a specific reacting group, body, or object instead of 'the room,' and no more than two crowd-reaction sentences across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:159-645 still use 'the room' as the grammatical subject. Fail if the hearing continues to default to 'the room' for most collective beats.

[FBR-003] Pass if the revised spans still preserve Lin's case but now contain at least two moments where procedure or pressure forces Lin to restart, redirect, or answer more narrowly before reclaiming ground; fail if Lin, Voren, and Yaret still share the same polished argumentative cadence or if the scene reads smoothly enough to forget it is a contested hearing.

[FBR-009] Pass if 7634-7636 now sounds unmistakably like Jo under pressure while 8010-8012 remains the single preserved stillness beat in the chapter and reads as Lin-specific rather than transferrable authorial shorthand; fail if Jo still reacts through generic stillness language or if the chapter adds another stillness default.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:289-295 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:403-500 clearly separates speaker texture on the page: Mira argues through record/process/cost language, while Lin's answers hinge on sensory recall, domestic detail, or concrete courtroom experience before any abstract claim. Fail if adjacent rebuttals still end on thesis-like maxims in both voices.
findings.evidence: The hearing climax starts to collapse Mira and Lin into the same polished, thesis-forward courtroom register at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:289, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:295, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:407, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:445, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:491, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:499.
findings.problem: The chapter's strongest scene is also where idiolect separation slips. Mira's argument turns more abstractly rhetorical than her usual cost-and-procedure exactness, and Lin's cross-exam answers escalate from embodied specificity into quotable thesis lines, pushing them toward philosophy-delivery rather than the style-bible mix of sensory recall, field-tested phrasing, and disarming literalism.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Mira's rebuttal at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:289 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:295 so she sounds like a claims worker arguing from process, evidence, and cost. Re-ground Lin's decisive turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:407, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:445, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:491, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:499 in bodily sensation, domestic habit, or immediate courtroom consequence, letting the panel infer the theme instead of hearing it as a slogan. Preserve Lin's stillness and literal-question stress pattern.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if none of the three cited beats opens with the exact phrase 'There it was' and each revised turn is announced through distinct syntax tied to its immediate object, pressure, or sensation. Fail if two or more of those moments still rely on the same opener.
findings.evidence: The exact reveal formula recurs at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:481, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:537.
findings.problem: The narration uses the exact 'There it was' setup three times to announce recognition beats. By the third use, a pivotal turn is arriving through recycled sentence machinery instead of a fresh local perception, which makes the chapter's biggest reversals feel patterned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the reveal beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:481, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:537 so each lands through a different sentence shape and a different local trigger: better wording in the hearing, steel-tag property language in cross, and the post-ruling care vacuum in recess.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the cited crowd-reaction beats are rewritten to name a specific reacting group, body, or object instead of 'the room,' and no more than two crowd-reaction sentences across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:159-645 still use 'the room' as the grammatical subject. Fail if the hearing continues to default to 'the room' for most collective beats.
findings.evidence: Collective reaction repeatedly gets routed through 'the room' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:257, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:367, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:453, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:495, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:559, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:645.
findings.problem: The hearing keeps personifying 'the room' as a single reacting organism. Used this often, the shorthand starts to flatten a chapter that should draw pressure from specific publics: reporters, plant workers, clinic staff, officers, and benches full of people with different stakes. The civic texture gets less particular each time the collective is collapsed back into one generic body.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:257, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:367, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:453, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:559, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:645, replace several 'the room' reactions with sharper, faction-specific noticing: which bench moves, which workers react, which reporters lean in, which officials stiffen, which objects carry the pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised spans still preserve Lin's case but now contain at least two moments where procedure or pressure forces Lin to restart, redirect, or answer more narrowly before reclaiming ground; fail if Lin, Voren, and Yaret still share the same polished argumentative cadence or if the scene reads smoothly enough to forget it is a contested hearing.
findings.problem: The tribunal climax is powerful, but the cross-examination overconcentrates applause-line rhetoric. Lin, Voren, and even the room land too many detachable thesis statements in sequence, so the scene starts reading as staged argument rather than pressured testimony under hostile procedure. That costs volatility exactly where the book needs its sharpest dramatic uncertainty.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and roughen snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7970-8024 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8052-8058. Keep Lin's intelligence and moral force, but break some answers with objections, pauses, restarts, and more evidentiary or sensory re-entry. Push Voren and Yaret harder on chain, contamination, and scope so Lin's strongest answers feel earned against active pressure rather than queued for quotation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if 7634-7636 now sounds unmistakably like Jo under pressure while 8010-8012 remains the single preserved stillness beat in the chapter and reads as Lin-specific rather than transferrable authorial shorthand; fail if Jo still reacts through generic stillness language or if the chapter adds another stillness default.
findings.problem: The full-book stillness pattern appears across 6 chapters and 8 instances. This chapter uses it twice: Jo at line 7634 and Lin at line 8010. KEEP Lin's 8010 beat because it matches Lin's canonical stress tell under direct dehumanization; REPLACE Jo's 7634 beat because it hands Jo the novel's generic shock posture instead of her own command-comic crisis response.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7634-7636, REPLACE Jo's stillness with an active Jo-specific response rooted in command, joke, logistics, or protective aggression. At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8010-8012, KEEP Lin's stillness but do not heighten it further with any new generic freeze phrasing elsewhere in the chapter. Let Jo move; let Lin hold.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
